title: argyranthemum frutescens subsp. canariae
intro: Argyranthemum frutescens subsp. canariae, also known as Canary Island daisy or marguerite, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Canary Islands, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Argyranthemum frutescens subsp. canariae are believed to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
